title: The Belmont Jewelry Heist
summary: On the night of March 15, 2024, Belmont Fine Jewelers on Rue de Rivoli was burglarized. Approximately 2.3 million euros in diamonds and watches were stolen. The alarm system was disabled at 2:47 AM using an insider code. Security footage shows a single masked figure entering through the back door. The lock showed no signs of forced entry. Three suspects have been brought in for questioning: Martin Lefèvre, a former employee fired 3 months prior; Antonin Morel, a mechanic and locksmith with a prior B&E conviction; and Claire Music, a night security guard who was seen near the store just before the break-in.
key_facts: Break-in occurred between 2:30 AM and 3:15 AM on March 15, 2024
Alarm was disabled using an insider code
No forced entry — lock was picked or opened with a key
Martin Lefevre was terminated on December 12, 2023 for insubordination
Lefevre had access to alarm codes during his employment
A white van was seen near the store at 2:20 AM
Stolen goods: 47 diamonds, 12 luxury watches, total value 2.3M euros
Lefevre's fingerprint was found on the back door handle
Lefevre claims he hasn't been near the store since December
Cell tower data places Lefevre's phone in the area between 1:00 AM and 4:00 AM
Antonin Morel owns a white van matching the description
Morel has a prior conviction for breaking and entering (2019)
Morel recently deposited 8,000 euros in cash
Morel's phone was turned off between 1:00 AM and 5:00 AM that night
Claire Music was seen on CCTV near the store at 2:35 AM during her patrol
Music works as a night guard at a building 200 meters from the store
evidence: Security footage (masked figure, no face visible)
Disabled alarm log showing insider code used
Fingerprint match on back door handle (Lefèvre)
Cell tower data placing Lefèvre's phone in the neighborhood
White van seen at 2:20 AM matching Morel's vehicle
Morel's bank records showing unexplained cash deposit
CCTV footage of Claire Music walking near the store at 2:35 AM
Music's building badge logs (to be verified)
